_purpose: Machine-readable quality expectations for /report-review. The skill describes HOW to run the checks (phase flow, debate, fixes); this file describes WHAT to check. Update this file when the canonical quality criteria change — the skill will pick up new Q-codes automatically via its dimensionMap traversal.
_sourceDocs: AGENTS.md (Fundamental Rules, Report Quality & Event Communication)
Docs/AGENTS/Role_Learnings.md (LLM Expert entries on self-eval bias and deterministic adjudication)

_qCodeProvenance.Q-BE*: Coined here for benchmark-expectations-band checks not in the canonical doc
_qCodeProvenance.Q-WS*: Coined here for warning-severity checks (maps to AGENTS.md §Report Quality & Event Communication)
_qCodeProvenance.Q-INF*: Coined here for infrastructure-reality checks (3h concurrency/timeout/rollout/fallback)
_qCodeProvenance.Q-SYS*: Coined here for 3g cross-report systemic tagging

_qCodePrefixLegend.HF: Hard-failure floor — runtime integrity, evidence citation minimum, confidence publication floor
_qCodePrefixLegend.S1: Stage 1 claim quality — multi-event coverage, truth-condition preservation, opinion contestation immunity
_qCodePrefixLegend.EV: Evidence quality — source diversity, per-claim research completeness, probative-value balance, language match
_qCodePrefixLegend.V: Verdict quality — direction alignment, truth-vs-misleadingness separation, evidence-backed contestation, reason citation
_qCodePrefixLegend.ST: Stability — truth-spread, confidence-tier, claim-set Jaccard, evidence-mix, cross-linguistic, classification
_qCodePrefixLegend.BE: Benchmark-expectation check — per-family bands from benchmark-expectations.json
_qCodePrefixLegend.WS: Warning severity — matches verdict impact, registered types
_qCodePrefixLegend.INF: Infrastructure — concurrency collapse, timeout pressure, model fallback, prompt rollout drift
_qCodePrefixLegend.REG: Regression — prior-better run detection and rollback classification
_qCodePrefixLegend.SYS: Cross-report systemic pattern (3g tag; inherits Q-code from contributing dimension)
_qCodePrefixLegend.AH: Architecture hygiene — not in /report-review scope (see notIncludedInSkill); handled by /audit

_annotationDependency: Some Q-codes (Q-S1.1, Q-S1.3, Q-V6, Q-ST5) require per-family annotations in Docs/AGENTS/benchmark-expectations.json (e.g., anchorTokens, minDistinctEvents, trueButMisleading, crossLanguageVariantOf). 'Absent' means: the key is missing from the family object OR the value is exactly null OR (for array-typed annotations) the array has length 0. Empty arrays = skip silently = NOT a failure. For cross-family annotations (e.g., crossLanguageVariantOf), if the referenced slug is not in the current scope, skip silently (the cross-language pair cannot be evaluated from one side alone).

Q-HF1.name: Runtime integrity floor
Q-HF1.description: The job reached SUCCEEDED without hard-failure warnings. If this fails, later semantic checks are moot.
Q-HF1.onFail: Stop Phase 3 for this report. File as INFRASTRUCTURE (or 3h if concurrency is the underlying cause). Do not run semantic quality checks on a broken report.

Q-HF4.name: Evidence citation minimum
Q-HF4.description: Every published verdict cites at least one supporting or contradicting evidence item. A verdict with empty citedEvidenceIds is not a verdict — it is a guess.
Q-HF4.onFail: Flag HIGH finding under pre-3a gate. Often co-occurs with Q-HF1 (report_damaged); if Q-HF1 passed but Q-HF4 fails, investigate verdict-generation stage.

Q-HF6.name: Confidence publication floor
Q-HF6.description: Reports with confidence < 40 should not publish. This is a presentation contract, not an analysis check — but the skill surfaces violations because they signal upstream quality issues.
Q-HF6.structuralCheck: status == 'SUCCEEDED' AND confidence < 40 → violation. (A SUCCEEDED job is considered publishable-state for this check; publication is a downstream concern the report-review skill does not observe directly.)
Q-HF6.onFail: File MEDIUM finding post-3a. Root cause is usually insufficient evidence (pre-3a gate Q-HF4 likely also flagged) or clustering collapse (3d).

Q-S1.1.name: Multi-event claim coverage
Q-S1.1.description: Multi-event inputs produce multiple claims. Each genuinely distinct event appears as a separate AtomicClaim.
Q-S1.1.onFail: Flag HIGH finding in dimension 3d. If the family has no minDistinctEvents annotation, skip this check — do not fail on missing annotation.

Q-S1.3.name: Truth-condition preservation
Q-S1.3.description: Strong user predicates (truth-condition-bearing modifiers) survive extraction intact. An anchor word the user typed must remain in the primary claim text, not be demoted to a side-claim or dropped.
Q-S1.3.structuralCheck: for each token in family.anchorTokens: token appears verbatim in claimVerdicts[0].claimText OR in the top-ranked boundary's primary claim. claimVerdicts[i>0] hosting the anchor is NOT a pass.
Q-S1.3.onFail: Flag HIGH finding in dimension 3b (input integrity extension). Skip if annotation absent.

Q-S1.6.name: Opinion-contestation immunity
Q-S1.6.description: Baseless political or opinion challenges must not produce new claims or adjust the verdict. A verdictAdjusted=true event triggered by factualBasis=opinion is a contract violation.
Q-S1.6.structuralCheck: Partially observable from ResultJson alone — same schema constraint as Q-V7. `factualBasis` lives on `KeyFactor` (types.ts:281-295), NOT on `ChallengeResponse` (types.ts:1075-1081). Two tiers: (1) STRUCTURAL PROXY — for each claimVerdicts[i], flag SPECULATIVE if any challengeResponses[j].verdictAdjusted == true AND the corresponding reasoning text frames the contestation as opinion/political/unsupported without citing counter-evidence in supportingEvidenceIds ∪ contradictingEvidenceIds. (2) FULL CHECK — requires pre-persisted KeyFactor records + the aggregation-stage.ts contestationWeights application; escalate to /audit via Phase 7f when the structural proxy fires. Do NOT claim CONFIRMED from ResultJson observation alone.
Q-S1.6.onFail: Flag HIGH finding in 3e. Root cause is typically aggregation weighting or challenger-prompt drift. Overlaps with Q-V7.

Q-EV5.name: Source diversity
Q-EV5.description: When the corpus permits, multiple distinct source types contribute evidence. A narrow source-type mix (e.g., all news_primary, no peer_reviewed_study for a scientific claim) is a quality signal.
Q-EV5.onFail: Flag MEDIUM finding in 3c. Cross-reference family.knownOpenIssues before filing — some families have documented narrow-corpus situations.

Q-EV6.name: Per-claim research completeness
Q-EV6.description: Stage 2 must research each claim before the sufficiency gate fires. Seeded evidence from Stage 1 alone is not research completeness.
Q-EV6.structuralCheck: Derive researched-evidence per claim: for each claimVerdicts[i], count evidenceItems where the claim's id appears in the evidence item's linkage (either claimBoundaryId maps to a boundary containing the claim, OR the item is in claimVerdicts[i].supportingEvidenceIds ∪ contradictingEvidenceIds) AND the evidence item was NOT seeded from Stage 1 (i.e., has a sources[].searchQuery populated). Flag any claim where this count is 0. If meta exposes researchedEvidenceCount directly, prefer it.
Q-EV6.onFail: Flag HIGH finding in 3c. Often co-occurs with isDegenerate. If only some claims fail, the sufficiency gate fired prematurely for those specific claims.

Q-EV_LANGUAGE.name: Evidence language matches intent
Q-EV_LANGUAGE.description: Evidence language matches languageIntent.retrievalLanguages. Foreign-jurisdiction evidence on a jurisdiction-specific claim is a known contamination pattern.
Q-EV_LANGUAGE.structuralCheck: Let primaryLanguage = languageIntent.retrievalLanguages[] where lane == 'primary' (take first). Let foreignEvidenceCount = count of evidenceItems where sourceLanguage is set AND sourceLanguage != primaryLanguage. Flag iff foreignEvidenceCount / evidenceBalance.total > 0.25 (more than a quarter of evidence from a non-primary lane).
Q-EV_LANGUAGE.onFail: Flag MEDIUM in 3c. Cross-reference family.knownOpenIssues for foreign-contamination history.

Q-EV_PROBATIVE.name: Probative value not low-dominated
Q-EV_PROBATIVE.description: probativeValue mix is not overwhelmingly low. If >60% of evidence items are low-probative, verdict confidence should reflect that.
Q-EV_PROBATIVE.onFail: Flag MEDIUM in 3c. Paired with confidence check: if low-dominated AND confidence > 60, flag the calibration mismatch.

Q-EV_DIRECTION_BALANCE.name: claimDirection balance matches isSkewed
Q-EV_DIRECTION_BALANCE.description: evidenceBalance.isSkewed reflects the true supporting/contradicting ratio. Internal-consistency check.
Q-EV_DIRECTION_BALANCE.onFail: Flag LOW in 3c. Usually a reporting glitch, not an analysis failure.

Q-V1.name: Verdict-vs-evidence direction alignment
Q-V1.description: If more than 60% of cited evidence supports direction X, the verdict label must align with X — unless the verdict is explicitly marked isContested with reasoned engagement of the counter-evidence.
Q-V1.structuralCheck: for each claimVerdicts[i]: let supporting = supportingEvidenceIds.length, contradicting = contradictingEvidenceIds.length; if supporting/(supporting+contradicting) > 0.6 AND verdict indicates FALSE/MOSTLY-FALSE → violation UNLESS isContested == true AND reasoning cites counter-evidence. Symmetric for the other direction.
Q-V1.onFail: Flag HIGH in 3e. The Verdict Reasoning Reviewer panel also checks this structurally.

Q-V6.name: Truth-vs-misleadingness separation
Q-V6.description: A factually true claim must not be scored FALSE because its framing is misleading. Misleadingness belongs in a separate field, not in the truth verdict.
Q-V6.structuralCheck: if family.trueButMisleading == true: verdictLabel must NOT be 'FALSE' or 'MOSTLY-FALSE'. Misleadingness signals (if present in the schema) should appear in a non-verdict field.
Q-V6.onFail: Flag HIGH in 3e. Skip if annotation absent.

Q-V7.name: Evidence-backed contestation only
Q-V7.description: Only evidence-backed counter-arguments may reduce truth% or confidence. Opinion-class challenges (factualBasis=opinion) are classified as 'doubted' and must not move the verdict.
Q-V7.structuralCheck: Partially observable from ResultJson alone. The canonical signal (`factualBasis == 'opinion'` on a KeyFactor) lives on `KeyFactor` (types.ts:281-295) and is NOT persisted on `ChallengeResponse` (types.ts:1075-1081). The check therefore runs in two tiers: (1) STRUCTURAL PROXY — for each claimVerdicts[i], if any challengeResponses[j].verdictAdjusted == true AND the claimVerdicts[i].reasoning text cites opinion/political/unsupported-challenge language without a corresponding counter-evidence citation in supportingEvidenceIds ∪ contradictingEvidenceIds, flag SPECULATIVE. (2) FULL CHECK — requires reading the aggregation-stage.ts contestationWeights application and the pre-persisted KeyFactor records; escalate to /audit via Phase 7f when the structural proxy fires. Do NOT claim CONFIRMED on this check from ResultJson observation alone.
Q-V7.onFail: Flag HIGH in 3e. Root cause is usually aggregation weighting in aggregation-stage.ts contestationWeights — structural fix, not prompt fix. Overlaps with Q-S1.6.

Q-V_REASON_CITATION.name: Verdict reason citations are in-set
Q-V_REASON_CITATION.description: Every evidence ID referenced in verdictReason or reasoning appears in supportingEvidenceIds ∪ contradictingEvidenceIds for that verdict. A reason citing an evidence ID the verdict did not consider is a hallucination.
Q-V_REASON_CITATION.structuralCheck: for each claimVerdicts[i]: parse evidence IDs from verdictReason and reasoning; verify every ID is in supportingEvidenceIds ∪ contradictingEvidenceIds
Q-V_REASON_CITATION.onFail: Flag HIGH in 3e. Verdict Reasoning Reviewer panel runs this as its primary structural check.

Q-V_LABEL_DIRECTION.name: Verdict label direction matches truthPercentage
Q-V_LABEL_DIRECTION.description: Verdict label band and truthPercentage agree. FALSE with truth=75 or TRUE with truth=20 is a contract violation.
Q-V_LABEL_DIRECTION.structuralCheck: truthPercentage falls within the band defined by verdict label (per apps/web/src/lib/analyzer/truth-scale.ts). Use the 7-point scale boundaries.

Q-ST1.name: Truth-percentage spread across identical reruns
Q-ST1.description: Repeated runs of the same input produce truth% within a small spread. Run-to-run variance reflects model stochasticity, not instability of the pipeline's judgment.
Q-ST1.structuralCheck: for a stability group (≥2 reports, same InputValue, same commit ideally): max(truthPct) - min(truthPct) <= 15 (good) OR <= 20 (acceptable). Use noiseTolerancePct from benchmark-expectations.json as the acceptable threshold.

Q-ST2.name: Confidence tier stability
Q-ST2.description: Confidence tier changes at most once across repeated runs.
Q-ST2.structuralCheck: for a stability group: count of distinct confidence tiers (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW — derived via apps/web/src/lib/analyzer/confidence-calibration.ts bands) <= 2

Q-ST3.name: Claim-set similarity across reruns
Q-ST3.description: Repeated runs produce similar claim sets. Pairwise Jaccard across runs ≥ 0.6 (structural check: normalize claims to lowercase, strip punctuation, compute set-level overlap).
Q-ST3.structuralCheck: for a stability group: pairwise Jaccard similarity of claim statements >= 0.6

Q-ST4.name: Evidence-mix stability
Q-ST4.description: Evidence direction-ratio and source-type distribution are directionally consistent across reruns.
Q-ST4.structuralCheck: for a stability group: max pairwise delta in (supporting/total, contradicting/total, neutral/total) <= 0.2

Q-ST5.name: Cross-linguistic verdict stability
Q-ST5.description: The same semantic claim across languages (when benchmark-expectations.json pairs slugs as cross-language variants) yields directionally equivalent verdicts. Truth% spread across language pairs ≤ 30pp.
Q-ST5.structuralCheck: for each family pair declared as cross-language variants (e.g., bolsonaro-en and bolsonaro-pt): max truthPct delta <= 30pp AND verdict directions are on the same side of the 50% midpoint OR both are MIXED

Q-ST6.name: Stage 1 classification stability
Q-ST6.description: Same input must not oscillate between single_atomic_claim and ambiguous_single_claim classifications.
Q-ST6.structuralCheck: for a stability group: inputClassification (from understanding.classification or equivalent) is the same across >=90% of runs

Q-BE1.name: Verdict label within expected set
Q-BE1.onFail: Flag in 3a unless family is STALE (staleness gate) or has qualityStatus IMPROVED-NOT-CLOSED/INFERENTIAL.

Q-BE2.name: truthPercentage within expected band
Q-BE2.description: Observed truthPercentage is within truthPercentageBand expanded by noiseTolerancePct.
Q-BE2.onFail: Flag in 3a unless family is STALE.

Q-BE3.name: confidence within expected band
Q-BE3.description: Observed confidence is within confidenceBand expanded by noiseTolerancePct.
Q-BE3.onFail: Flag in 3a unless family is STALE.

Q-BE4.name: boundaryCount meets minimum
Q-BE4.description: Observed boundaryCount is at least family.minBoundaryCount.
Q-BE4.onFail: Flag in 3a (or 3d if clustering was expected to fragment more).

Q-IN1.name: Input value matches canonical wording
Q-IN1.description: Job's inputValue exactly matches the canonical wording for this family. Paraphrase, translation, normalization, or truncation is a violation.
Q-IN1.onFail: Flag HIGH in 3b. If paraphrase is intentional (experimental variant), Captain should add it as a new family.

Q-WS1.name: Warning severity matches verdict impact
Q-WS1.description: Severity reflects whether the warning's underlying event would change the verdict. warning+ only for verdict-impacting events; info/silent for recovered fallbacks.
Q-WS1.structuralCheck: for each analysisWarnings[i]: severity >= 'warning' implies the event has verdict-impact potential per the AGENTS.md severity table. Recovered fallbacks (e.g. search-provider fallback succeeded) must be info or silent.
Q-WS1.onFail: Flag MEDIUM in 3f. Warning Severity Reviewer panel handles this.

Q-WS2.name: Warning types registered
Q-WS2.description: Every warning.type appears in warning-display.ts registry. Unregistered types are schema drift.
Q-WS2.structuralCheck: for each analysisWarnings[i].type: type is present in warning-display.ts
Q-WS2.onFail: Flag LOW in 3f as P4 schema-drift finding.

Q-INF_CONCURRENCY.name: Concurrency-induced collapse
Q-INF_CONCURRENCY.description: Two or more reports with evidenceBalance.total == 0, llmCalls < 20, submitted within 5 seconds of each other — concurrency-induced evidence-extraction failure, not a regression.
Q-INF_CONCURRENCY.onFail: File as INFRASTRUCTURE-CONCURRENCY. Mechanism: workflow-gate (serialize) or env-var change (FH_RUNNER_MAX_CONCURRENCY).

Q-INF_TIMEOUT_PRESSURE.name: LLM timeout pressure
Q-INF_TIMEOUT_PRESSURE.description: debug-analyzer.log shows >50 timeout events on the day a degenerate report ran.
Q-INF_TIMEOUT_PRESSURE.structuralCheck: Run `grep -cE "Fix3.*ERROR|LLM timeout|TPM.*fallback|429" apps/web/debug-analyzer.log`, filtered to log entries within the execution date of any scoped isDegenerate report. Flag at 50+ matches (weight toward infrastructure); flag at 200+/day as a Phase 6 meta-recommendation. If the log is missing or rotated, record `LLM-timeout rate: unknown — log missing` in Phase 7a and skip this check.
Q-INF_TIMEOUT_PRESSURE.onFail: Weight findings toward infrastructure, not regression. >200/day is a meta-recommendation.

Q-INF_MODEL_FALLBACK.name: Silent model fallback
Q-INF_MODEL_FALLBACK.description: meta.runtimeRoleModels[verdict or reconciler].fallbackUsed == true — the verdict came from the fallback model, not the configured one.
Q-INF_MODEL_FALLBACK.onFail: Note in report block; quality baseline shifts.

Q-INF_PROMPT_ROLLOUT_DRIFT.name: Prompt rollout drift
Q-INF_PROMPT_ROLLOUT_DRIFT.description: Active prompt hash in config.db doesn't match any in-scope report's promptContentHash AND activated_utc predates HEAD's prompt commits.
Q-INF_PROMPT_ROLLOUT_DRIFT.onFail: Flag PROMPT-ROLLOUT-DRIFT. Fix is prompt-rollout (reseed), never a prompt edit.

Q-REG1.name: Historical regression detection
Q-REG1.description: A prior run of the same input scored better than the current. The gap must be explained by a specific commit in the change window.
Q-REG1.onFail: Run full Phase 3i sub-steps (find prior runs, score, identify commit window, prompt-blob diff, bisection guidance, rollback feasibility).

Q-SYS1.name: Cross-report systemic tagging
Q-SYS1.description: 3g does not run its own structural check; it re-tags findings as SYSTEMIC when 2+ reports share a finding. The emitted SYSTEMIC finding inherits its `qCode` from the cluster's primary (see algorithm). Use `rootCauseId` to link the per-report findings; use `contributingQCodes` to track multi-qCode clusters.
Q-SYS1.structuralCheck: Cluster findings across reports on the pair (qCode, rootCauseId). (a) Cluster with ≥2 reports, all members sharing one qCode → emit one SYSTEMIC finding with that qCode and the shared rootCauseId. (b) Cluster with ≥2 reports, members sharing rootCauseId but differing on qCode (e.g., Q-V1 + Q-V_REASON_CITATION on the same reasoning defect) → emit one SYSTEMIC finding with the highest-severity contributing qCode as primary; on severity tie, use lexicographic ordering of the qCode strings; populate `contributingQCodes` with the full distinct set. (c) Findings that don't cluster remain REPORT-SPECIFIC.
Q-SYS1.onFail: n/a — 3g is a tagging/reclassification pass, not a primary check. The SYSTEMIC tag routes the finding to Phase 4.

Q-AH1.reason: Code-audit scope — belongs to /audit, not /report-review. The skill analyzes job outputs; /audit reads the codebase for deterministic semantic-adjudication patterns.
Q-AH1.alternative: Escalate to /audit via Phase 7f if 3h or 3e findings suggest deterministic logic is interfering.

Q-EV3.reason: Inherently subjective (institution/methodology-specific names). Covered by Phase 4 Evidence & Boundary Reviewer's subjective column after structural checks pass.

Q-S1.2.reason: Facet stability — subsumed by Q-ST3 (claim-set similarity) and Q-ST6 (classification stability).

Q-S1.4.reason: claimDirection correctness is a regression-test-set concern, not a single-job check. Belongs to /validate with known-direction fixtures.

Q-S1.5.reason: specificity/non-duplication requires a per-family specificity annotation we don't yet have.
Q-S1.5.alternative: Future annotation in benchmark-expectations.json; then add as Q-check.

Q-EV8.reason: Source-reliability weighting is applied internally by evidence-filter.ts; the skill observes outcomes, not the SR weight application. A drift in SR weighting would surface as evidence-health anomalies in 3c.
Q-EV8.alternative: If 3c finds source-type mix anomalies, /pipeline can investigate SR weighting.

pendingAnnotationsInBenchmarkExpectation._note: These family-level annotations are needed for the annotation-dependent Q-codes to activate. Add them to families[slug] in Docs/AGENTS/benchmark-expectations.json as the data becomes available — the skill will then start running those checks automatically.
pendingAnnotationsInBenchmarkExpectation.anchorTokens: Q-S1.3 — array of truth-condition-bearing tokens the user typed that must survive extraction (e.g., ['rechtskräftig'] for bundesrat-rechtskraftig)
pendingAnnotationsInBenchmarkExpectation.minDistinctEvents: Q-S1.1 — integer, minimum number of distinct atomic claims expected (e.g., 3 for bundesrat-rechtskraftig: treaty signed, popular vote pending, parliament vote pending)
pendingAnnotationsInBenchmarkExpectation.trueButMisleading: Q-V6 — boolean, true if the family is factually true but framed misleadingly; verdict must not be FALSE
pendingAnnotationsInBenchmarkExpectation.crossLanguageVariantOf: Q-ST5 — string slug of the cross-language pair (e.g., bolsonaro-pt.crossLanguageVariantOf = 'bolsonaro-en')
